heading: Small town environmental planning
label: 6908 Small town environmental planning
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Federal Facility Compliance Act of 1992, and not as part of the Solid Waste Disposal Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (hereafter referred to as the “Administrator”) shall establish a program to assist small communities in planning and financing environmental facilities. The program shall be known as the “Small Town Environmental Planning Program”.

children.heading: Small Town Environmental Planning Task Force
children.label: b Small Town Environmental Planning Task Force

children.text: The Administrator shall establish a Small Town Environmental Planning Task Force which shall be composed of representatives of small towns from different areas of the United States, Federal and State governmental agencies, and public interest groups. The Administrator shall terminate the Task Force not later than 2 years after the establishment of the Task Force.

children.label: A
children.text: identify regulations developed pursuant to Federal environmental laws which pose significant compliance problems for small towns;

children.label: B
children.text: identify means to improve the working relationship between the Environmental Protection Agency (hereafter referred to as the Agency) and small towns;

children.label: C
children.text: review proposed regulations for the protection of the environmental and public health and suggest revisions that could improve the ability of small towns to comply with such regulations;

children.label: D
children.text: identify means to promote regionalization of environmental treatment systems and infrastructure serving small towns to improve the economic condition of such systems and infrastructure; and

children.label: E
children.text: provide such other assistance to the Administrator as the Administrator deems appropriate.

Not later than 6 months after , the Administrator shall publish a list of requirements under Federal environmental and public health statutes (and the regulations developed pursuant to such statutes) applicable to small towns. Not less than annually, the Administrator shall make such additions and deletions to and from the list as the Administrator deems appropriate.October 6, 1992

children.label: C
children.text: Trade, municipal, and other associations that the Administrator determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Small Town Ombudsman
children.label: d Small Town Ombudsman
children.text: The Administrator shall establish and staff an Office of the Small Town Ombudsman. The Office shall provide assistance to small towns in connection with the Small Town Environmental Planning Program and other business with the Agency. Each regional office shall identify a small town contact. The Small Town Ombudsman and the regional contacts also may assist larger communities, but only if first priority is given to providing assistance to small towns.

children.label: A
children.text: environmental benefits and liabilities of a multi-media permitting program;

children.label: B
children.text: the potential of using such a program to coordinate a small town’s environmental and public health activities; and

children.label: C
children.text: the legal barriers, if any, to the establishment of such a program.

Within 3 years after , the Administrator shall report to Congress on the results of the evaluation performed in accordance with paragraph (1). Included in this report shall be a description of the activities conducted pursuant to subsections (a) through (d).October 6, 1992

children.heading: “Small town” defined
children.label: f “Small town” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “small town” means an incorporated or unincorporated community (as defined by the Administrator) with a population of less than 2,500 individuals.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: g Authorization
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated the sum of $500,000 to implement this section.
